items.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave

items.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
items.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions

items.title: Defence seeks acquittal for former naval commander in ARA San Juan submarine trial
items.description: Prosecutors on Monday urged a five-year prison sentence for Claudio Villamide, commander of the submarine force at the time of the tragedy; Defence lawyers request acquittal, arguing there is no evidence linking his actions to the deaths of the crew Leer más

items.title: Could neo-Nazi youth, or 'active clubs,' have played a role in Belfast riots?
items.description: In the wake of racist mob violence in Northern Ireland, there has been attention on "active clubs," a global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center activities around mixed martial arts.

items.title: Two former Moroccan politicians jailed in 'Escobar of the Sahara' drug case
items.description: Throughout the proceedings, the defence argued there was no material evidence linking the former officials to the alleged crimes, saying the prosecution relied largely on the testimony of the convicted trafficker.

items.title: Middle East: Trump announces new Doha talks, Iran demurs
items.description: The US and Iran have agreed to refrain from hostilities after a recent flareup, according to Washington, with US President Trump anouncing new talks will be held in Doha. Iran has yet to confirm this. DW has more.

items.title: Newborn baby rescued from rubble of Venezuela quake
items.description: Video shared on social media shows rescuers bringing the infant out to applause in the hardest-hit city of La Guaira, north of the capital Caracas. Leer más

items.title: From trips to unexplained wealth – the scandals that led to Manuel Adorni's resignation
items.description: Former Cabinet chief steps down after months of mounting political and judicial scrutiny – here’s how we got here. Leer más

items.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, say officials
items.description: Pakistani security forces carried out a ground operation along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border, followed by strikes against militant hideouts and safe havens, killing 29 fighters

items.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
items.description: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

items.title: In foreigners’ eyes: What galvanizes young people in a changing China
items.description: Between 2020 and 2024, official figures show a gradual increase in membership of the Communist Party of China (CPC), with more than 80% of new members under the age of 35. The post In foreigners’ eyes: What galvanizes young people in a changing China appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Fathers wake up, By Ayo Akerele
items.description: History has repeatedly shown that the rise or collapse of civilisations is often tied to the quality of the men who occupy strategic positions of influence. Adolf Hitler’s devastating impact on Germany, and ultimately the world, was not achieved by his charisma alone, but by his deliberate recruitment of highly skilled men who aligned themselves […] The post Fathers wake up, By Ayo Akerele appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Germany: At least 6 killed in shooting in Stade
items.description: Police said the deadly Stade shooting was likely an "extended family tragedy" triggered by a custody dispute, with the state interior minister describing it as "extremely cold-blooded act of violence."

items.title: DR Congo says 1,307 Ebola cases confirmed, including 377 deaths
items.description: Outbreak spreads to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the CAR, according to a media report.

items.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
items.description: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

items.title: EU gets tough on China as trade imbalance stokes deindustrialisation fears
items.description: Talks between EU trade commissioner and Chinese commerce minister come as bloc seeks to limit influx of Chinese imports.

items.title: On the ground in Venezuela: 'All the buildings have collapsed'
items.description: France 24's Maxime Pluvinet reports from La Guaira state, one of the areas hardest hit by the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24. "All the buildings have collapsed" he says, whle "Venezuelans are still waiting for news from their loved ones trapped under the rubble".

items.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
items.description: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.

items.title: How ships are risking ‘safe’ passage through the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: A plan to divert shipping through an alternative corridor off the Omani coast to avoid the threat of mines in the Strait of Hormuz has come under scrutiny after two attacks on ships in recent days. Though Iran and Oman agreed to engage in talks on Monday on the strait's management, the incidents illustrate the complexity of maintaining navigation through the crucial waterway, which remains at the forefront of the standoff between Tehran and Washington.

items.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
items.description: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

items.title: 'You are Canadian heroes'
items.description: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.
items.source: the forgotten hosts making World Cup history

items.title: Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence and Milei’s ethics
items.description: Milei has a militant and narrow world view that he’s tied to a vision that integrates AI and tech billionaires. Those same players have a global agenda that they will pursue, and Argentina will only be part of that plan for as long as it is useful for them. Leer más

items.title: Hungary: Magyar's 'Operation Purgatory' tackles Orban system
items.description: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

items.title: Adorni resigns, blames media and clears path for Santilli
items.description: The political death throes of Javier Milei's third Cabinet chief lasted longer than expected. There is relief in the Casa Rosada after a pincer movement involving all libertarian factions, PRO, Patricia Bullrich and the UCR. Diego Santilli is preparing to lead the cabinet and repair the damage. Leer más

items.title: Helicopter spotlight a 'shining light' for stranded fisherman
items.description: After more than four hours floating in rough seas, 77-year-old John "Stinker" Clarke was starting to lose hope. Then he saw a light from above.

items.title: South Africa on edge ahead of anti-migrant protests
items.description: Anti-immigrant groups have set June 30 as an unofficial deadline for undocumented migrants to leave, with protests planned across South Africa. Large numbers of security forces have been deployed. Follow DW for more.

items.title: Pair get temporary ban on keeping dogs after RSPCA inspection
items.description: Two people have been convicted and disqualified from owning dogs for five years after a severely emaciated dog was rescued by RSPCA inspectors and nursed back to health.

items.title: Javier Milei taps lifetime politician Diego Santilli to lead his Cabinet after scandal
items.description: Javier Milei once vowed to eradicate entrenched politicians like Diego Santilli, yet he turned to him as a candidate and later named him interior minister. Now, the former PRO politician is Argentina's new Cabinet chief. Leer más

items.title: Iran war supercharges pivot to renewable energy
items.description: The Iran war and high oil and gas prices have supercharged the adoption of renewables and EVs across the world. Some experts wonder if this year could be the peak of oil demand.

items.title: Can Thailand, Cambodia unlock massive oil and gas reserves?
items.description: An estimated $300 billion in oil and gas reserves is sitting under overlapping territory in the Gulf of Thailand. Cooperation on its extraction is complicated by political tensions over a separate land border dispute.

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: Thai May factory output falls 0.8% y/y, weaker than forecast
Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
items.description: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

items.title: Founder of Asian super-app Gojek sentenced to years in jail for corruption
items.description: The case against Gojek founder Nadiem Makarim relates to a laptop deal made during his term as Indonesia's education minister.

items.title: Trump calls U.S. housing bill 'a big yawn,' presses voting curbs
items.description: His comments highlight a growing tension ‌between White House attempts to overhaul U.S. elections and address concerns over consumer affordability, both core parts of his Republican Party’s campaign for November midterm elections

items.title: Myanmar jade mine landslide kills five, 15 missing
items.description: Hpakant has also witnessed vicious combat in recent months between the Myanmar military and opposition forces, vying for control of jade mining operations

items.title: US-India trade deal: The key sticking points
items.description: Both India and the US have signaled that the first phase of a landmark trade deal is close. DW looks at the main challenges in getting a deal past the finish line.

items.title: South Africa: Legal foreigners fearful as anti-immigrant deadline looms
items.description: With increasing attacks on African immigrants, even those living legally in the country are scared ahead of an unofficial deadline for illegal foreigners to leave the country.

items.title: Paraguay knock Germany out of World Cup 2026 on penalties
China's Meituan says new AI model trained on domestic chips
items.description: Meituan says the performance of its new large language model, LongCat-2.0, is comparable to Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro, which was released in February.

items.title: South Africa braces for anti-migrant protests
items.description: Tensions are rising ahead of planned anti-migrant protests on June 30, as South Africa's government warns against violence. Analysts say deep social and economic crises are fueling unrest and xenophobia.

items.title: South Africa races to ease growing tensions ahead of planned anti-immigration marches
items.description: More than 15,000 Malawian nationals have already been processed to leave the country, with thousands more waiting in temporary camps as authorities work to repatriate foreign nationals before an unofficial June 30 deadline set by citizen-led groups.
